name: Freezer Jam
recipeIngredient: Apricot: 1 3/4 pounds (2 1/2 cups) apricots, pits removed, no peeling
Blueberry: 2 1/2 pints fresh or frozen blueberries (3 cups crushed), stems removed
Cherry (sour): 1 1/2 pounds (2 cups) cherries, stems and pits removed
Peach: 2 pounds fresh or frozen peaches (2 1/4 cups chopped), peeled, pitted
Raspberry: 3 pints fresh or frozen raspberries (3 cups crushed)
Strawberry: 1 quart fresh or frozen strawberries (2 cups crushed)
recipeInstructions:
1. Measure fruit into a large bowl along with lemon juice (if required).
2. Measure sugar into a medium bowl and level off the excess.
3. Stir sugar into the fruit, setting aside for 10 minutes while stirring occasionally.
4. Prepare pectin mixture: In a small saucepan, combine 1 box SURE-JELL Fruit Pectin with 3/4 cup water. Bring to a boil on high heat, stirring constantly until boiling point is reached, then continue stirring for 1 minute. Remove from heat.
5. Stir the pectin mixture into the fruit mixture and stir continuously until sugar dissolves completely and no graininess remains, about 3 minutes. A few sugar crystals may still be present.
6. Pour jam quickly into clean plastic containers, leaving at least 1/2 inch of space at the top.
7. Wipe container edges and secure lids tightly.
8. Allow to cool at room temperature for 24 hours to set. If not fully set after 24 hours, refrigerate as indicated in the recipe.
9. Store in refrigerator up to 3 weeks for immediate use.
10. Freeze remaining containers up to one year. Thaw in the refrigerator before using.
